section_title: INTRODUCTION TO NEW DAY OPENING GAP (NDOG)
content:
This module will discuss the New Day Opening Gap (NDOG). Similar to the New Week Opening Gap, the
openings for the New Day Opening Gaps are not random. We will examine the New Day Opening Gap
opening price.

Looking at May 3rd, 2023, you can observe the 6 PM restart after an hour break in Futures Trading. On a
five-minute chart:
- The opening price is the very first tick or traded price.
- This price is not random; it's offered to the market, and the first filled trade at that point starts the
beginning of the New Day Opening Gap.

section_title: DEFINING THE NDOG CLOSING PRICE AND DAILY FORMATION
content:
We then look at the closing price from the previous session. Closing prices are also not a random price
event.
- The New Day Opening Gap forms daily, from Monday through Thursday, as the difference between the
closing price of the previous session (e.g., 5 PM session) and the reopening of the Futures Market one
hour later (e.g., 6 PM).
- On Friday, an NDOG does not typically apply due to the New Week Opening Gap covering the period
between Friday's close and Sunday's open.
- Note on Time Zones: While charted as May 3rd in New York local time, the period around midnight into
the 4th (which would start May 4th in local time) is often factored as part of May 4th trading on platforms
like TradingView. The important aspect is the two price points forming the new day opening gap.

section_title: EXTENDING THE NDOG INTO FUTURE PRICE ACTION
content: The New Day Opening Gap can be extended into future price action, providing valuable reference points.

section_title: NDOG AS DYNAMIC SUPPORT AND RESISTANCE
content:
- INITIAL OBSERVATIONS
Consider an NDOG created from Wednesday's 6 PM (New York local time). When this gap is extended
through Thursday and Non-Farm Payroll Friday, careful attention should be paid to how price respects it.
- Price frequently interacts with the NDOG as support or resistance.
- It often consolidates around the gap, finding equilibrium between its high and low.
- Price gravitates around the NDOG, offering buy-side then sell-side before rallying or selling off into
inefficiencies.

section_title: NDOG AS DYNAMIC SUPPORT AND RESISTANCE
content:
- CONTINUED EXAMPLES
The NDOG continues to exhibit significant price action interaction:
- It creates resistance multiple times.
- It provides support, even after sweeping stops before rallying.
- It acts as resistance again.
- It offers support, digging down into the gap one more time before sending price higher, as observed post
- Non-Farm Payroll data.

- MEANINGFUL SEPARATION
Similar to the New Week Opening Gap, which can be observed over many weeks and months (e.g., 60
days), the New Day Opening Gap also has specific utilization criteria.
- The NDOG may not always form with a meaningful separation between the 5 PM close and the 6 PM new
trading session open.
- If there is no meaningful separation between these two price points, the NDOG should not be utilized for
analysis.
- Gaps of only one or two ticks are generally not preferred, even if price can respect them. A more
substantial separation is desired.
- While there isn't a specific number of handles or points, the separation should preferably be more than
one handle to be considered meaningful.

section_title: TIMEFRAME RELEVANCE AND PERSISTENCE OF THE NDOG
content:
The NDOG data is carried forward and analyzed throughout the entire week.
- Once the trading week closes, that specific New Day Opening Gap (and its two associated levels) is no
longer considered relevant for the new trading week.
- For a new week, the focus shifts to utilizing the very first meaningful New Day Opening Gap that forms,
extending it throughout that week.
- For example, if on ES there wasn't any other meaningful NDOG except one early in the week, observe
how it was treated into Thursday and Friday's trading.
- Closing the gap is not a point of abandoning it. The area is referred back to, and price often gravitates
around it.
- These NDOG levels will be impactful support or resistance levels for the week. They represent specific
PD Arrays where repricing occurs.

section_title: NDOG CONFLUENCE WITH FAIR VALUE GAPS (FVG) AND OPTIMAL TRADE ENTRY
content:
(OTE)
The NDOG often shows confluence with other ICT concepts.
- The New Day Opening Gap low (where it opens at 6 PM), when extended forward, can align with a Fair
Value Gap (FVG), which is a buy-side imbalance/sell-side inefficiency.
- A drop down into this confluence point (NDOG low and FVG) can represent an Optimal Trade Entry (OTE).
- When price closes in that gap and retouches the New Day Opening Gap low, it can find support and rally.
- Price may hit this area again, taking out sell-side liquidity below relative equal lows, especially during
sessions like the London session on Friday.

section_title: NDOG CONFLUENCE WITH BULLISH BREAKER AND CANDLE BODY SIGNIFICANCE
content:
Observe price action in relation to the NDOG and other structures:
- After rallies, if price forms a low-high-lower low sequence, it can create a Bullish Breaker.
- Price trading into a Fair Value Gap followed by a Bullish Breaker can lead to rallies.
- When price digs back into the breaker, pay close attention to the candle bodies. The Wicks may do the
damage, but the body tells the story.
- If candle bodies are unwilling to close below a significant swing or the New Day Opening Gap top high, it
indicates underlying strength.
- Digging down into an old New Day Opening Gap High can provide support, sending price higher in
confluence with Fair Value Gaps and Order Blocks.

section_title: NDOG AS A SUPPORTING FACTOR AND SCALPING OPPORTUNITY
content:
New Day Opening Gaps serve as a supporting factor or resistance to an existing trading idea derived from
other PD Arrays or analysis concepts.
- It is not a blind indicator or a panacea, but rather an additional layer of confirmation.
- If the New Day Opening Gap has not yet repriced to its high (or low, in the case of a gap higher), it often
will.
- Example of a Scalp Trade:
- When price opens, trades down, and consolidates around the low of the gap.
- Trading up to the high of that New Day Opening Gap can be a very easy, 'bread and butter' type scalp.
- This often presents itself as an Asian session trade, suitable for those who can study or tape read during
those hours.
